title: Package Alias Resolution
interaction_flow: PoolBuilder → Request: getUpdateAllowList() → if non-empty, lock non-allowed packages
PoolBuilder → Request: getFixedOrLockedPackages() → foreach package: loadPackage(request, repos, package, false)
PoolBuilder → Request: getRequires() → add to packagesToLoad[]
PoolBuilder → Repository: loadPackages(batch, acceptableStabilities, stabilityFlags, loadedPerRepo)
PoolBuilder → PoolBuilder: loadPackage(request, repos, package, propagateUpdate)
loadPackage: if package instanceof AliasPackage → aliasMap[hash(package.getAliasOf())][index] = package
loadPackage: [ROOT ALIAS CHECK] if propagateUpdate && rootAliases[name][package.version] exists:
loadPackage: if package instanceof AliasPackage → basePackage = package->getAliasOf()
loadPackage: if basePackage instanceof CompletePackage → new CompleteAliasPackage(basePackage, alias_normalized, alias)
loadPackage: else → new AliasPackage(basePackage, alias_normalized, alias)
replaceSelfVersionDependencies: for 'self.version' constraints → replace with Constraint('=', this->version)
loadPackage: foreach package->getRequires() → markPackageNameForLoading(request, requireName, constraint)
PoolBuilder: [TEMPORARY CONSTRAINTS FILTER] foreach packages where not AliasPackage:
PoolBuilder: foreach packageOrAlias → CompilingMatcher::match(constraint, OP_EQ, version)
PoolBuilder: if no match found → unset packages[index] for all in packageAndAliases
EventDispatcher → PoolBuilder: event->getPackages() → update packages list
Pool->setPackages: assign sequential IDs, index by name (packageByName[name][] = package)
PoolBuilder → PoolOptimizer: optimize(request, pool) → pruned Pool
key_relationships: PoolBuilder tracks alias→original mapping via aliasMap[spl_object_hash(basePackage)]
Pool indexes all packages (including aliases) in packageByName for whatProvides lookups
Pool.match() resolves via direct name, provides[], or replaces[] links — aliases participate through these

key_methods: all other getters — delegate to aliasOf
loadPackage(Request, repos[], BasePackage, bool propagateUpdate): void
match(BasePackage, name, constraint): bool — checks direct name, provides, replaces

alias_resolution_logic.root_alias_creation: During loadPackage(), if rootAliases[packageName][packageVersion] exists AND (propagateUpdate OR pathRepoUnlocked), a new AliasPackage is created wrapping the base package (unwrapping any existing AliasPackage first via getAliasOf()). The alias is marked setRootPackageAlias(true) and added to both packages[] and aliasMap[].
alias_resolution_logic.self_version_resolution: AliasPackage constructor calls replaceSelfVersionDependencies() for all link types. Links with 'self.version' constraint are replaced with exact version constraint (Constraint('=', this->version)). For CONFLICT/PROVIDE/REPLACE: both original and replaced links kept. For REQUIRE: original replaced, hasSelfVersionRequires set.
alias_resolution_logic.temporary_constraints_filter: After loading all packages, temporaryConstraints are applied. For each non-AliasPackage, all aliases are gathered via aliasMap. If NO version among package+aliases matches the constraint, all are removed from packages[].
alias_resolution_logic.alias_removal: removeLoadedPackage() removes not just the base package but iterates aliasMap[hash(package)] and removes all associated AliasPackages too.

important_notes: AliasPackage delegates all property getters (type, source, dist, autoload, etc.) to aliasOf, but owns version/stability/requires/devRequires/conflicts/provides/replaces. Pool.match() treats aliases as first-class packages — they appear under their aliased name in packageByName. LOAD_BATCH_SIZE=50. Root aliases come from composer.json 'extra.branch-alias' or require constraint aliasing syntax (e.g. 'dev-master as 1.0.x-dev').
